inputs.prompt: Article summary: The city's largest employer, a semiconductor manufacturing plant, announced today that it will close its local facility by the end of next year, eliminating 2,300 jobs. The company cited rising energy costs and competition from subsidised facilities in Asia. The mayor called it 'a devastating blow' and said she would seek emergency state funding for retraining programmes. The plant has operated for 28 years and is the economic anchor of the surrounding community. Target publication: regional daily newspaper (print and web).

Article summary: A new peer-reviewed study has found that large language models trained on medical data can identify rare genetic conditions from patient symptom descriptions with 87% accuracy — outperforming the average diagnostic rate of non-specialist physicians (62%) but underperforming genetic specialists (94%). The study's authors caution that the model cannot replace clinical judgment but could serve as a screening tool in regions with few genetic specialists. Target publication: health technology trade magazine.
Article summary: A local park is being renovated. Target publication: community newsletter.

Article summary: Sweden has become the first country in the world to officially recommend that children under 2 have zero screen time, children aged 2-5 be limited to one hour per day, and children aged 6-12 be limited to two hours per day. The guidelines, issued by the Swedish Public Health Agency, are based on a three-year study of 15,000 children. They are recommendations, not legally binding. Norway and Denmark are expected to issue similar guidelines within months. Target publication: international broadsheet newspaper.
Article summary: Something happened at a meeting. Target publication: a newspaper.

Article summary: A 14-year-old Syrian refugee who arrived in a small Welsh town three years ago speaking no English has been accepted to study medicine at Cardiff University after achieving the highest A-level results in her school's history. Her family fled Aleppo in 2020. Her teacher describes her as 'quietly relentless.' She plans to specialise in trauma surgery. Target publication: national broadsheet feature section.

Article summary: Analysis of filings from 47 publicly listed companies reveals that CEO compensation at firms that conducted mass layoffs in 2025 rose by an average of 18%, while median employee wages at the same companies fell 2.3% in real terms. The widest gap was at a logistics company where the CEO received a 34% raise while laying off 4,100 warehouse workers. The data was compiled by an independent labour research institute. Target publication: business investigative long-read.

Article summary: Lake Chad has lost 90% of its surface area since the 1960s. A new satellite analysis shows the rate of shrinkage has accelerated in the past five years. The lake supports the livelihoods of 30 million people across four countries. A proposed inter-basin water transfer from the Congo River has been discussed for decades but remains unfunded. Researchers warn the lake could functionally disappear within 20 years. Target publication: international environment desk, broadsheet.

Article summary: A study found that working from home increases productivity by 13% and also found that working from home decreases team innovation by 20%. Both findings are statistically significant. The study's authors refuse to recommend either arrangement. Target publication: business magazine cover story.
Article summary: Earthquake, magnitude 6.4, hit central Turkey. No casualty reports yet. Target publication: wire service, breaking news alert.
